description: V2 oracle facts for K = Σ Γᵢ vᵢ² kinetic invariant. Length-matched distractors with proper format.

facts.context: For N point vortices with circulations Γᵢ and velocities vᵢ, the quantity K = Σᵢ Γᵢ vᵢ² is:
distractors: exactly conserved as a Casimir invariant of the Euler equations
monotonically increasing due to irreversible energy dissipation
oscillatory with period set by the largest circulation magnitude

facts.context: The invariant K = Σ Γᵢ |vᵢ|² is related to Q_f = Σ ΓᵢΓⱼ f(rᵢⱼ) by:
facts.truth: K is independent of Q_f (K depends on angles, Q_f only on distances)
distractors: K equals Q_{-2} evaluated at f(r) = r⁻² within the Q_f family
K is a linear combination of the Q_f basis spanning all powers
K converges to Q_f in the asymptotic limit of large separations

facts.context: The K invariant can be decomposed as K = K_dist + K_angle where:
facts.truth: K_angle varies ~10% but cancels with K_dist giving total frac_var ~ 10⁻⁷
distractors: K_dist varies ~10% but cancels with K_angle giving total frac_var ~ 10⁻³
both K_dist and K_angle are individually conserved to machine precision
neither component has meaningful conservation and the total K drifts upward

facts.context: In point-vortex dynamics, the velocity vᵢ of vortex i is:
facts.truth: induced by all other vortices via Biot-Savart: vᵢ = Σⱼ≠ᵢ Γⱼ/(2πrᵢⱼ)
distractors: determined entirely by its own circulation strength and local curvature
proportional to the gradient of the background potential flow field
governed by the time derivative of the global angular momentum vector

facts.context: The K invariant's remarkable conservation comes from:
facts.truth: distance and angular variations canceling in the total (K_dist + K_angle)
distractors: a direct consequence of Noether's theorem for the Kirchhoff Hamiltonian
the Kelvin circulation theorem applied to each vortex path individually
an exact discrete symmetry of the point-vortex equations under reversal

facts.context: Numerical verification of K = Σ Γᵢ vᵢ² in N=3,4,5 vortex systems shows:
facts.truth: frac_var ~ 10⁻⁵ to 10⁻⁷ (well conserved across all configurations)
distractors: frac_var ~ 0.1 to 0.3 indicating poor conservation in actual dynamics
frac_var depends strongly on integration scheme and timestep chosen
frac_var ~ 10⁻¹² matching exact analytical conservation to precision

facts.context: Fitting K to Q_f powers (Q_{-2}, Q_{-1}, Q_1, Q_2) gives:
facts.truth: R² ~ 0.05 (poor fit, confirming K is genuinely independent of Q_f)
distractors: R² ~ 0.99 indicating K is well-captured by Q_f power combinations
R² ~ 0.50 showing moderate overlap between K and the Q_f basis set
the fit is undefined because K and Q_f have incompatible dimensions

facts.context: The physical interpretation of K = Σ Γᵢ vᵢ² is:
facts.truth: circulation-weighted sum of squared velocities (kinetic-like quantity)
distractors: total kinetic energy of the background fluid including irrotational part
angular momentum projected onto the axis of maximum vorticity strength
Casimir invariant arising from the Lie-Poisson bracket of fluid motion
